steps.title: Identify the antecedent and what it requires
steps.content: The blank fills "The Eiteljorg Museum counts pieces by S'eiltin in ______ impressive collection." The pronoun refers to The Eiteljorg Museum — singular and an institution, not a person. The pronoun must be singular and non-personal.

steps.title: Eliminate three, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — its: Singular possessive for non-persons. The Museum and its collection — agreement holds. ✓
  • Choice B — her: Possessive for female persons. The Eiteljorg Museum is an institution, not a person. ✗
  • Choice C — it's: Contraction of "it is." Substituting gives "the Museum counts pieces in it is impressive collection" — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — their: Plural possessive. The antecedent (The Eiteljorg Museum) is singular. ✗
A is correct.
